params.text:
Welcome to Catheter Care

Welcome to Catheter Care. Many of the people you support live with a urinary catheter, and the everyday care around it has a direct effect on their comfort, dignity and safety. This course helps you understand what a catheter is, why people have one, and how clean, careful, person-centred support helps prevent infection and other problems.

  • Understand: what a urinary catheter is and why it is used.
  • Prevent: how good practice reduces the risk of infection.
  • Escalate: when and how to report concerns to a registered nurse.

Awareness only — know your limits

Catheter care is a delegated clinical task. This course raises your awareness only. You must never insert or remove a urinary catheter — that is the role of a registered nurse or doctor. You may only carry out delegated catheter care, such as emptying a drainage bag or supporting meatal hygiene, if you have had task-specific training, been assessed as competent, and the task has been delegated to you by a registered nurse and set out in the person's care plan.

This course does not authorise you to carry out catheter care without task-specific training, assessed competence and delegation by a registered nurse. If anything looks or feels abnormal, you stop and escalate to the nurse. Throughout, remember that your two most important skills are noticing changes and reporting them promptly.
